audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Joseph in Potiphar's house, YHWH with Joseph, success/prospering language, temptation/refusal, false accusation, prison, and favor with the prison chief.

verses.restored: Joseph had been brought down to Egypt, and Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh, captain of the guard, an Egyptian man, bought him from the Ishmaelites who had brought him down there.
The Eternal was with Joseph, and he became a successful man, and he was in the house of his Egyptian master.
His master saw that the Eternal was with him and that the Eternal made all that he did succeed in his hand.
Joseph found favor in his eyes and served him, and he appointed him over his house and gave all that he had into his hand.

From the time he appointed him over his house and all that he had, the Eternal blessed the Egyptian’s house because of Joseph; the blessing of the Eternal was on all that he had, in the house and in the field.
He left all that he had in Joseph’s hand, and with him there he knew nothing except the bread he ate. Joseph was beautiful in form and beautiful in appearance.
After these things, his master’s woman lifted her eyes toward Joseph and said, Lie with me.
He refused and said to his master’s woman, Look, with me here my master does not know what is in the house, and he has given all that he has into my hand.
No one is greater in this house than I, and he has withheld nothing from me except you, because you are his woman. How then could I do this great evil and sin against the Divine?
Though she spoke to Joseph day after day, he did not listen to her to lie beside her or to be with her.
One day he came into the house to do his work, and none of the men of the house were there inside.
She seized him by his garment, saying, Lie with me. He left his garment in her hand, fled, and went outside.
When she saw that he had left his garment in her hand and fled outside,
she called to the men of her house and spoke to them, saying, See, he has brought us a Hebrew man to laugh at us. He came to me, and I cried out with a loud voice.
When he heard that I lifted my voice and cried out, he left his garment beside me, fled, and went outside.
She kept his garment beside her until his master came home.
She spoke to him according to these words, saying, The Hebrew servant whom you brought to us came to me to laugh at me.
When I lifted my voice and cried out, he left his garment beside me and fled outside.
When his master heard the words his woman spoke to him, saying, This is what your servant did to me, his anger burned.
Joseph’s master took him and put him into the prison, the place where the king’s prisoners were confined, and he was there in the prison.
But the Eternal was with Joseph and stretched covenant-kindness to him, and gave him favor in the eyes of the chief of the prison.
The chief of the prison gave into Joseph’s hand all the prisoners who were in the prison; whatever they did there, he was the one doing it.
The chief of the prison did not look into anything in Joseph’s hand, because the Eternal was with him, and whatever he did, the Eternal made it succeed.
